name: Genoise
recipeIngredient: 6 Eggs, at room temperature or warmer
recipeInstructions:
1. Warm the bowl of an electric mixer and beat the eggs with the sugar and vanilla at high speed until stiff peaks form when the beater is withdrawn. Depending on the power of the mixer, this should take from 5 to 30 minutes. Scrape the sides of the bowl with a rubber spatula occasionally to ensure ingredients are well blended.
2. Grease two 9-inch or three 8-inch cake pans that are 1 1/2 inches deep. Line the pans with waxed paper and grease it. Melt the butter and allow it to cool to lukewarm. Set oven temperature to moderate (350°F) and place the rack in the lower third of the oven.
3. Divide the flour into six to eight portions and sift over the egg mixture one portion at a time, folding gently with a rubber spatula after each addition.
4. Add butter gradually, about a teaspoon at a time, and fold it in completely but gently.
5. Pour batter into prepared pans and bake for 35-40 minutes. The cake is done when it rebounds slightly when gently pressed in the center.
6. Remove cakes from pans onto a cooling rack and let cool.
